fields.name: Students Robe
fields.description: A basic robe of an academic student. Helps provide focus in study.

fields.name: Clerics Cloth
fields.description: The cloth of a faithful disciple. Makes one more endearing to those around them.

fields.name: Sack
fields.description: A sack. Put it on your head.

fields.name: Leather Armor
fields.description: Functional leather armour for a nimble fighter.

fields.name: Academics Robe
fields.description: Robes of a learned mage with some experience.

fields.name: Lightning Wrap
fields.description: A fighters wrappings that crackles with lightning.

fields.name: Mages Armor
fields.description: The battle armor of a practiced mage.

fields.name: Battle Preachers Armor
fields.description: The battle armor of a faithful of the art of war.

fields.name: Four Dragon Priest Robes
fields.description: Ritual robes of a dragon priest. Contains sigils of protection corresponding to the four common types of dragon.

fields.name: Light Tailored Chain
fields.description: A light chain to protect against some attacks. Requires a small amount of strength and skill to move around in.

fields.name: Heavy Chain
fields.description: A heavier chain which provides more bleed negation, but requires greater strength.

fields.name: Assassins Padding
fields.description: A durable armor of flexible padding. Comfortable and offers nice protection.

fields.name: Light Alloy Armor
fields.description: A well made alloy of metals, fashioned into movable and light weight armor.

fields.name: Spellswords Chain Mail
fields.description: A chain mail armor with runes ascribed along its silver chains. Can provide great focus and clarity, along with magical protection.

fields.name: Spellswords Heavy Gambeson
fields.description: A large gambeson which bolsters its wearers health and protects from cold and poisons.

fields.name: Protectors Bone Weave
fields.description: A weave of many strange bones. Provides great status protection and an increase to its wearers hardiness.

fields.name: Protectors Multi-Scale Weave
fields.description: A weave of ancient scales, all of a different sheen of color. Provides a wide range of elemental resistance, as well as a boost to its wearers focus.

fields.name: Buff Scholars Wrappings
fields.description: A heavy wrapping which burdens the body and disciplines the mind.

fields.name: Basic Studded Armor
fields.description: A heavy but movable armor made of steel studs and hard leather.

fields.name: Mixed Plate Armor
fields.description: A well designed armor of plates, studs and metal fittings.

fields.name: Plate Mail Armor
fields.description: A heavy but movable armor made of steel studs and hard leather.

fields.name: Crystalline Plate
fields.description: Pristine crystal plates arranged in a dazzling armor.

fields.name: Fulgur Scale Plate
fields.description: Charged scales arranged in a movable, but sturdy heavy armor.

fields.name: Silver Knight Armor
fields.description: Beautifully crafted and solid armor. Worn by those accomplished in the physical art of battle.

fields.name: Spellswords Pale Plate
fields.description: A pale plate of both protection and focus. Imbues unwavering stillness to a calm mind.

fields.name: Paladins Plate Mail
fields.description: A resplendent plate mail of gold and vibrant inlays. Bestows the confidence of a true faithful knight.

fields.name: Stone Slab Armor
fields.description: Heavy slabs of stone placed together in an array. Takes strength and skill to move effectively in this armor, but can provide extremely high physical protection.

fields.name: The Platinum Wall
fields.description: A massive full set of glimmering platinum armor. The strength required to move in this armor can only strike fear in those who come to face battle with its wearer.

fields.name: Tormentor's Chain
fields.description:
Once a crude instrument of agony and despair, forged in the darkest depths of torment, the Tormentor's Chain now binds its wielder to an unyielding fate. Stolen from the very hands of the Tormentor, this heavy, rusted chain hums with the echoes of suffering long past. While its cold steel still bears the marks of countless souls broken by its weight, it is no longer an instrument of subjugation. Wielded with grim resolve, the chain now serves as both a haunting memento of torment survived, and a testament to the wielder's unrelenting endurance. Those who grasp it will feel its weight, but also the fire it ignites within. It is a chain that both shackles and strengthens.

Gain +2 Sanity and whenever a creature targets you with a damaging attack/spell/ability more than once in a round of combat, you may reduce the damage by 1d6 and heal (before or after taking the damage of the attack) for [D Vitality Scaling]. This effect can be applied only once per round of combat.

fields.name: The Zand Clover
fields.description:
A small clover flower with four petals. Attuned by keeping it within a worn piece of
clothing. Each time you make a skill check with a result of 1 on the die, one of the petals
shrivels and falls off. Has no effect until all four petals fall off, at which point this artifact
bestows the effects of the Lucky feat of destiny, but instead it procs the effects when you roll
a skill check with a result of 1 on the die instead of 20. This stacks with the Lucky feat itself
if you already have it, but they both increase each others DC for increasing a skill. If this
artifact loses attunement then its effects are permanently lost (restoring any Lucky DC by the
appropriate amount) and its petals grow back.

fields.name: Grant’s Leaching Vial
fields.description:
A small crystalline vial filled with a shimmering blue and red liquid. Attuned by dropping
it into your already attuned recovery flask. Whenever you drink a crimson flask it
restores HP as though it was one upgrade higher than normal and, if drank during combat for
the first time, you gain +1 Max AP for the duration of that combat. Whenever you drink a
cerulean flask it restores FP as though it was one upgrade lower than normal (minimum flask
upgrades of +0 and maximum of +9 as usual). The vial can only be removed from an empty
recovery flask.

fields.name: Armond’s Rapport
fields.description:
An old copy of a Rapport spellbook, but with strange notes and modifications which make it
impossible to learn the spell from. Attuned by reading and memorizing its odd contents
and holding it in your inventory while having the Rapport spell attuned. Causes
all of your casts of the Rapport spell to become permanent.

fields.name: Earrings of Bell
fields.description:
A pair of unassuming earrings no different from choir bells in appearance. Attuned by
placing them on your ears. If you die with 5 stacks of undying and succeed on your
undying check while this artifact is attuned, then your undying is increased to 6, the earrings
disappear, and a circular thorny mark is branded onto your neck. Your undying can then be
further increased to a new maximum of 7 but the threshold for your undying check remains as
though you still had 5 stacks. When a person with the mark on their neck turns undead and
is slain, a new pair of these earrings appears upon them. The thorny mark cannot be removed
by any normal means

fields.name: Liz’s Ring of Rings of Sacrifice
fields.description:
A bracelet with multiple indents where rings of sacrifice may be added. Attuned by wearing
it around the wrist. Allows you to wear up to three of the ring of sacrifice adornments in
only one ring/adornment slot.

fields.name: Recording Tome
fields.description:
A large brown tome of unremarkable make. Attuned by writing your name in light chalk
upon its spine. Records into itself the first (Att mod) spells that you clearly perceive being
cast. This allows you to learn from the tome as though it was a collection of those spells
respective spellbooks. After one attempt at reading through to learn each entry, they fade
from the pages. Perceiving another spell while the tome is full erases the earliest entry and
replaces it.

fields.name: Ruby’s Gloves
fields.description:
Red gloves of rubbery leather. Attuned by wearing over both hands. Gain +1 range to
the success of all of your parry attempts (+ or -1 off of the correct AP guess still acts as a
successful parry).

fields.name: Multiplied Fashioned Steel
fields.description:
A strange dull knife embedded in a handle with multiple gears and switches. Attuned by
wearing it in an exposed equipment belt. While attuned all thrown items multiply when
thrown, allowing them to fly toward two different target grid spaces at once

fields.name: Condensed Blood Sphere
fields.description:
A crystal ball of calcified blood which smells of rot. Can be created from the deep flesh of
an old amalgamated creature. Attuned by having it added as a jewel to your armor.
Whenever a creature within 40 feet is bled out you may regenerate 1d4 FP and you may
increase the primary damage die of your attacks by one level for one round of combat. The
bled out status effect on you is now automatically staunched on your following turn, as the
ball cause the blood to quickly clot.

fields.name: Condensed Pure Ice Crystal
fields.description:
A crystal ball of pure ice, which pulses with cool energy and never melts. Can be created from
the shed flesh of a powerful frostbitten creature. Attuned by having it added as a jewel
to your weapon. Whenever a creature is inflicted by frostbite while within 40 feet of you,
you may regain 2d6 HP. Inflicting frostbite on a creature with a melee weapon now allows you
to immediately make a reaction follow up light attack with that weapon, targeting the same
creature, for no AP cost. The frostbite status effect now only lasts for one round on you, as
the ball absorbs the cold around you.

fields.name: Runic Mural
fields.description:
A massive hand about 5 feet across with grey flesh and runes carved into it. Attuned by
eating it in its entirety, then vomiting it up after a couple of days in the form of
a much smaller hand which can be held on your person. When opened, palm up, in
a location it dampens and sometimes erases any reality altering effects within 50 feet. This
effect may take place regardless of it has been attuned

fields.name: Cursed eye
fields.description:
A blackened rock roughly the size of a human eye. Attuned by replacing one of your own
eyes with the rock. (Att mod) times per short rest allows you to see all equipment and
inventory of one creature within 20 feet.

fields.name: Cursed Tome
fields.description:
An ornate jeweled tome filled with what seems to be nonsensical runes. Attuned by carrying
on your person and re reading at least three days in a row at first light of day.
Every day at first light forces you to remove a knowledge point from one knowledge skill and
add it to another, but each day this must add to a different skill. If this causes any of your
knowledge skills to become negative or be increased to any number above 10, then the tome
disintegrates and you gain +1 to all of your knowledge skills except one.

fields.name: Cloth of a Herald Maidens blood
fields.description:
A small white cloth, stained with blood and impossible to clean. Attuned by holding it
against your flesh for one short rest. When given to another human to hold over at least
one bonfire short rest, it allows you to be aware of their location at any time as long as they
continue to hold the cloth. If they cloth is then given to another to hold over a short rest, its
effect is moved to the new person. If the cloth is removed from the person then you become
aware of it and the location of the cloth.

fields.name: Blooming Deathroot
fields.description:
A small shrivelled black root with a strange face blossoming from part of it. Flies and other
insects seem to spawn from its folds. Attuned by allowing it to pierce and hang from
your flesh. Grows roots in the direction of the nearest living creature at a rate of 1 inch per
hour. Can be cut to prevent overgrowth, but once it is attuned it will continue to grow from
the flesh until death

fields.name: Calcified Cursed Newborn
fields.description:
A stone-like and curled form of a cursed newborn. Attuned by having it on your person
for at least one week. Pulls in the hatred and ire of attackers and allows you to retaliate
with a cursed apparition. Once per short rest cause an area of effect attack which threatens
you to only target your grid space, but prevents you from dodging it. In the next two rounds
after doing so, cause any one of your attacks to also target another grid space in its range by
summoning a dark brown and yellow flaming apparition. After this artifact pulls 377 worth of
damage from attacks you have absorbed (before any resistance and reductions) it becomes a
purging stone item

fields.name: Cleansing Pate
fields.description:
A small shrivelled head. Attuned by holding the head in hand and verbally giving it
a compliment. (Att mod) times per short rest, for 5AP, you may blow gently into the base
of the heads skull to remove all poison and toxic build up from yourself, and also heal for the
total amount of reduced build up. Whenever this artifact is used, all willing and non hostile
creatures within 25 feet may also partake in its effects.

fields.name: Physical Glass Plate
fields.description:
A small square plate of almost completely transparent glass. The glass has exceptional strength
and is extremely difficult to break, unless it is touched at its exact center, at which point it
will shatter and disintegrate. Attuned by cleaning the plate around its edges for a
number of hours, at which point it will glow slightly while in your inventory. If the
glass is removed from your inventory it ceases to glow and becomes unattuned. If you pierce
physical tiers with any of your attack, then those attack pierce all physical tiers of resistance
on their targets.

fields.name: Wheel of Vengeful Souls
fields.description:
A wooden wheel about 2 feet in diameter which can be separated into two pieces connected
with a spoke. Attuned by spinning the two wheels in opposite directions while
holding the middle spoke. Once per long rest, When you reach 0 HP the wheel lets out a
number of screams of different voices and resets itself, at which point any and all recent status
conditions are reflected from you onto those that inflicted them, and your HP is increased to
full.

fields.name: Blissful Mind
fields.unlock_requirements: Resist 3 Sanity effects with a DC of a least 14
fields.description:
"Your mind relishes in pain inflicted upon you, your Sanity is further bolstered."
The sharp sting of suffering is no longer an adversary, but a strange ally. The chain whispers to the mind, turning agony into euphoria, letting the bearer transcend mortal frailty. The more pain inflicted, the stronger the resolve. Your Sanity grows with each wound, the very suffering you endure knitting your mind with newfound fortitude.

(Increases innate Sanity Bonus of the artifact by +2 and allows you to double that level/7 times per short rest to resist an effect)

fields.name: Faithless Mind
fields.unlock_requirements: Make 3 different creatures at the same time fail a Sanity check
fields.description:
"Once the tormented, now the tormentor. Creatures lose -2 Sanity on their roll to resist your Sanity effects."
In your hands, the chain becomes an extension of cruelty incarnate. What once was a tool of suffering has now become a conduit for your own twisted will. You have learned the art of causing insanity, bending the minds of others to your unyielding dominance. The touch of your torment brings madness, and creatures find their resistance fading under the weight of your unholy influence.

(Reduces enemy Sanity rolls by -2 to effects inflicted by you, other enemy creatures that can perceive what has transpired must make a DC = [Original Sanity Effect DC - 2] or be forced to make precision attacks against you for the duration of the round. This effect lingers until they beat the DC, which they can do one per round at the start of their turn.)

fields.name: One in All
fields.unlock_requirements: Survive an attack that would have killed you thanks to the Artifact's passive effect
fields.description:
"Death's grip is often narrowly averted, if averted at all."
The chain's bond with you deepens, entwining your very existence with its dark power. In the face of death, the line between life and oblivion grows ever thinner. Each time you near your end, the chain seems to pull you back from the precipice, stitching together what should have been lost. Your endurance is a reflection of the chain's resilience, a fragile yet unbreakable thread between life and death.

(Increases the innate healing scaling of the artifact to B scaling, you may double the healing value on the first attack that would have killed you per long rest. The healing and damage resistance provided by the artifact can now be used twice per round)

fields.name: Knight
fields.description: A warrior class balanced in physical stats, equipped with sword and shield for defensive combat. Knights are trained in traditional martial arts, combining solid vitality with reliable strength and dexterity. They favor medium armor and excel at protecting allies.

fields.name: Mercenary
fields.description: Agile fighter specializing in dual-wielded curved swords with high dexterity. Mercenaries are hired blades who rely on speed and endurance rather than brute force. They wear light armor and strike with precision and grace.

fields.name: Warrior
fields.description: Brutish fighter with high HP and strength, wielding a powerful axe. Warriors are hardy combatants who rely on raw physical power and endurance to overwhelm their foes. They boast the highest starting vitality of any background.

fields.name: Herald
fields.description: A balanced warrior-cleric with reach weapon and healing miracles. Heralds blend martial prowess with divine faith, wielding halberds to keep foes at bay while supporting allies with restorative magic.

fields.name: Cruel Veteran
fields.description: Experienced fighter combining melee prowess with arcane knowledge. Cruel Veterans have seen countless battles and learned to supplement their combat skills with sorcerous power, making them unpredictable opponents.

fields.name: Assassin
fields.description: Stealthy fighter blending precision attacks with sorcery. Assassins strike from the shadows, using both blade and spell to eliminate targets with ruthless efficiency. They balance dexterity with intelligence for deadly effect.

fields.name: Thief
fields.description: Nimble rogue with high dexterity and attunement, lowest HP pool. Thieves rely on speed, stealth, and cunning to survive. Though frail, their exceptional reflexes and magical aptitude make them formidable when played cleverly.

fields.name: Inquisitor
fields.description: Faith-focused warrior skilled in miracles and precise combat. Inquisitors hunt heretics and monsters with unwavering devotion, combining divine magic with swift swordplay to purge evil.

fields.name: Sorcerer
fields.description: Frail but powerful spellcaster focused on intelligence-based sorcery. Sorcerers dedicate themselves to the arcane arts, sacrificing physical resilience for unparalleled magical potency. They have the lowest starting HP but highest intelligence.

fields.name: Cleric
fields.description: Devoted priest specializing in faith-based miracles and support. Clerics serve as healers and divine champions, channeling their unwavering faith into powerful miracles that aid allies and smite the wicked.

fields.name: Pyromancer
fields.description: Balanced caster wielding fire magic requiring both intelligence and faith. Pyromancers harness the primal power of flame, requiring equal devotion to both arcane study and spiritual discipline. They blend melee capability with destructive fire magic.

fields.name: Curious Noble
fields.description: Scholarly aristocrat with high attunement and lowest strength, focused on magic. Curious Nobles are privileged individuals who pursue magical knowledge as a pastime. Physically weak but magically gifted, they can choose any school of magic to study.

fields.name: Wanderer
fields.description: Nomadic fighter with balanced stats wielding an exotic twinblade weapon. Wanderers are travelers who have honed their skills across many lands. They favor unique weapons and possess well-rounded capabilities suited to varied challenges.

fields.name: Deprived
fields.description: Blank slate character with perfectly balanced stats and minimal equipment. The Deprived represent those who begin with nothing, their destiny unwritten. With all stats equal, they can develop in any direction the player chooses.

fields.name: Chaotic Tarnished
fields.description: Chaotic origin character with randomized stats offering high variance gameplay. Those marked as Chaotic Tarnished have unpredictable destinies shaped by chance. Their starting HP equals twice their Vitality, and their capabilities vary wildly based on fortune's whim.
fields.special_rules: Choose one stat to be 15 (not including Endurance), then roll 6d2 six times and assign the results to the other six stats as you see fit. Your starting HP equals your Vitality stat multiplied by 2. If your Strength is 10 or less, start with light armor; if greater than 10, start with medium armor. Choose two weapons from the starting equipment list with stat requirements that you meet.

fields.name: Death Touched Ferno
fields.description: Can respawn in place on successful undying check via white ghostflame. Loses HP regeneration in darkness, instead takes HP damage in bright light.
fields.new_abilities: {"special_abilities": [{"name": "Ghostflame Respawn", "description": "Can respawn in place on successful undying check via white ghostflame", "type": "passive"}], "regeneration": {"hp": {"base": "-1d2/hour", "condition": "in bright light"}}}

fields.name: Skin Draped Ferno
fields.description: Can harvest skin from corpses for temporary bonuses. Half darkvision, Sanity becomes Precision, loses undying detection.
fields.new_abilities: {"vision": {"type": "half_darkvision", "range": 30}, "skill_bonuses": {"Precision": 2}, "special_abilities": [{"name": "Harvest Skin Mats", "description": "Can harvest skin from corpses for bonuses (+1 FP, +2 HP, creature-specific bonus per mat). Max (1 + level/5) mats, expire after 1 day", "type": "active", "max_capacity_formula": "1 + level/5", "duration": "1 day"}]}

fields.name: Geode Grme
fields.description: Hard crystalline skin. Replaces tier resistance with flat physical and magic reduction.
fields.new_abilities: {"resistances": [{"type": "physical", "flat": 1, "scaling": [{"level": 13, "flat": 2}, {"level": 24, "flat": 3}]}, {"type": "magic", "flat": 1, "scaling": [{"level": 13, "flat": 2}, {"level": 24, "flat": 3}]}]}

fields.name: Diamond Grme
fields.description: Clear crystalline eyes. Replaces tier resistance with +1 tiers to ALL resistible damage types when at or below 50% HP.
fields.new_abilities: {"special_abilities": [{"name": "All Resistance When Wounded", "description": "+1 tiers to ALL resistible damage types when \u226450% HP", "type": "passive", "condition": "hp_below_half", "scaling": [{"level": 17, "effect": "+2 tiers to all resistances"}]}]}

fields.name: Abyssal Darrow
fields.description: Darker skin with black pockmarks. Loses all sanity bonuses but gains perfect spatial perception of last creature with skin contact (lasts 10 hours).
fields.new_abilities: {"special_abilities": [{"name": "Perfect Spatial Perception", "description": "Gain perfect spatial perception of last creature with skin contact (10 hours)", "type": "passive", "duration": "10 hours"}]}

fields.name: Bleeding Eye Darrow
fields.description: Loses sanity bonuses. Can conjure invisible yellow flames that daze enemies.
fields.new_abilities: {"special_abilities": [{"name": "Conjure Yellow Flames", "description": "Conjure invisible yellow flames (25 ft range, 1 FP, once/short rest +1 at level 15). Creatures moving into flame make DC 12 sanity check or become dazed. Flame lasts 1 round, extendable for 1 FP/round", "type": "active", "cost": "1 FP", "usage": "once/short_rest", "range": "25 ft", "scaling": [{"level": 15, "effect": "2 uses per short rest"}]}]}

fields.name: Night Corvian
fields.description: Dark feathers with bone covering eyes. Full darkvision but only get FP regen at night or in darkness.
fields.new_abilities: {"vision": {"type": "darkvision", "range": 60}, "regeneration": {"fp": {"base": "1 + level/10 per hour", "condition": "at night or in darkness"}}}

fields.name: Broad Wing Corvian
fields.description: Large wingspan allows gliding and negates fall damage, but lose half darkvision and jump bonuses.
fields.new_abilities: {"movement": {"glide": "1 space down per 1 space horizontal", "fall_damage_reduction": 1.0}}

fields.name: Sand Wretch Locust Folk
fields.description: Yellowish coarse carapace. Loses the 1 HP survival ability.
fields.new_abilities: {"special_abilities": [{"name": "Bonus Knowledge", "description": "2 knowledge points per odd level (instead of 1)", "type": "passive"}, {"name": "Locust Drain", "description": "Heal for half damage dealt in one round", "type": "active", "usage": "once/long_rest", "scaling": [{"level": 4, "uses": 2}, {"level": 9, "uses": 3}, {"level": 16, "uses": 4}, {"level": 25, "uses": 5}]}]}

fields.name: Swarm Sentinel Locust Folk
fields.description: Hard brownish-yellow spiked carapace. +3 to lowest non-knowledge skill, add total knowledge to max HP. Locust Spines reflect damage.
fields.new_abilities: {"special_abilities": [{"name": "Skill and HP Bonus", "description": "+3 to lowest non-knowledge skill, add total knowledge to max HP", "type": "passive"}, {"name": "Locust Spines", "description": "Reflect HP lost as true damage to melee attacker", "type": "active", "usage": "once/long_rest", "scaling": [{"level": 18, "uses": 2}, {"level": 28, "uses": 3}, {"level": 35, "uses": 4}]}]}
fields.unlock_requirement: Sand Wretch Locust Folk level 15+ with Wretch Queen ritual

fields.name: Deep Earth Locust Folk
fields.description: Lose locust drain and bonus knowledge. Gain half darkvision and dark resistance that can heal when damage is fully negated.
fields.new_abilities: {"vision": {"type": "half_darkvision", "range": 30}, "resistances": [{"type": "dark", "flat": 2, "scaling": [{"level": 7, "flat": 3}, {"level": 13, "flat": 4}, {"level": 24, "flat": 5}]}], "special_abilities": [{"name": "Cannot Be One-Shot", "description": "Cannot be killed in one hit when at full HP (leaves at 1 HP instead)", "type": "passive", "condition": "at_full_hp"}, {"name": "Dark Damage Healing", "description": "When dark damage fully negated, heal that amount instead", "type": "passive"}]}

fields.name: Serpentine Dragonkind
fields.description: Snake-like appearance. Lose innate bonuses. Can grow/shed scales for physical resistance or movement. Enhanced abilities when submerged/drenched.
fields.new_abilities: {"special_abilities": [{"name": "Scale Shedding", "description": "Grow/shed scales for +1 tier physical resistance OR 1 AP free movement/turn. Both effects active when submerged/drenched (+2 tier physical, 2 AP movement at level 17)", "type": "active", "scaling": [{"level": 17, "effect": "When submerged: +2 tier physical, 2 AP movement"}]}, {"name": "Aquatic Movement", "description": "Move through water without penalty, hold breath 2x longer", "type": "passive"}]}

fields.name: Ancient Scale Dragonkind
fields.description: Petrified scales and 4 elemental wings. Lose elemental resistances but gain knowledge, FP regeneration in combat, and Wing Shockwave ability.
fields.new_abilities: {"skill_bonuses": {"World/History": 3}, "regeneration": {"fp": {"base": "1 FP per turn in combat"}}, "special_abilities": [{"name": "Four Hard Wings", "description": "4 hard wings (fire/frost/magic/dark/poison/lightning). Change wing type for 3 AP, once/short rest or at rest end", "type": "active", "cost": "3 AP", "usage": "once/short_rest"}, {"name": "Wing Shockwave", "description": "Hits all adjacent spaces for 1d6 damage or 1d4 status buildup", "type": "active", "usage": "once/short_rest", "scaling": [{"level": 13, "effect": "2 uses, 2d6 damage or 2d4 buildup"}, {"level": 21, "effect": "3 uses, 3d6 damage or 3d4 buildup"}, {"level": 30, "effect": "4 uses, 4d6 damage or 4d4 buildup"}]}]}
fields.unlock_requirement: Regular Dragonkind level 10+ with communion of 3+ dragon types

fields.name: Fire Beacon Demonkind
fields.description: Extreme fire affliction. Emit bright and dim light. Retaliation becomes a blinding effect instead of damage.
fields.new_abilities: {"special_abilities": [{"name": "Emit Light", "description": "Emit 10 ft bright light + 10 ft dim light", "type": "passive"}, {"name": "Blinding Retaliation", "description": "Retaliation becomes DC (10 + Att mod) perception or inflict impaired vision for 1 round", "type": "passive", "usage": "once/short_rest", "scaling": [{"level": 10, "effect": "DC 12 + Att mod"}, {"level": 20, "effect": "DC 14 + Att mod"}, {"level": 30, "effect": "DC 16 + Att mod"}]}]}

fields.name: Black Horned Demonkind
fields.description: Lesser fire, greater bone/horn affliction. Retaliation deals physical damage and armor durability loss.
fields.new_abilities: {"special_abilities": [{"name": "Durability Retaliation", "description": "Retaliation is physical damage + 1d2 armor durability loss", "type": "passive", "usage": "once/short_rest", "scaling": [{"level": 15, "effect": "1d4 armor durability loss"}]}]}

fields.name: Outer Vileblood
fields.description: Dark nebulous spirals in eyes/spine/arms. Bleed threshold becomes frost. Dark damage/frost creates nebulous voids instead of blood mists.
fields.new_abilities: {"thresholds": {"frost": 15, "scaling": [{"level": 17, "frost": 20}]}, "special_abilities": [{"name": "Nebulous Void Creation", "description": "Dark damage or frost buildup creates nebulous voids. Max (1 + level/10) voids active. Voids last 2 minutes", "type": "passive", "max_capacity_formula": "1 + level/10", "duration": "2 minutes"}, {"name": "Absorb Void for FP", "description": "Absorb void for 1d4 HP to regen 1d2 FP", "type": "active", "cost": "1d4 HP", "scaling": [{"level": 10, "effect": "1d4 FP"}, {"level": 20, "effect": "1d6 FP"}]}, {"name": "Void Pull", "description": "Void pulls creature 1 space if fail DC (10 + Att) athletics, 2 AP, once/round", "type": "active", "cost": "2 AP", "usage": "once/round"}]}

fields.name: Timeworn Vileblood
fields.description: Lose blood mist abilities (keep bleed threshold and sanity penalty). Half speed movement. Gain telepathy and short-range teleport.
fields.new_abilities: {"movement": {"speed_multiplier": 0.5, "special": "2 AP per 5 ft movement (half speed), cannot speak normally"}, "special_abilities": [{"name": "Telepathy", "description": "Telepathy (20 ft range)", "type": "passive", "range": "20 ft"}, {"name": "Teleport", "description": "Teleport 2 spaces for 1 AP, Att stat times per short rest", "type": "active", "cost": "1 AP", "usage": "Attunement times per short rest"}]}

fields.name: Oceanic Elden
fields.description: Twisted oceanic features. Lose abyssal detection and Bloodthirsty trait. Gain aquatic abilities and weapon feat when submerged.
fields.new_abilities: {"special_abilities": [{"name": "Weapon Proficiency Scaling", "description": "+1 weapon proficiency at levels 1, 9, 19, 28, 35", "type": "passive", "scaling": [{"level": 1, "effect": "+1 weapon proficiency"}, {"level": 9, "effect": "+1 weapon proficiency"}, {"level": 19, "effect": "+1 weapon proficiency"}, {"level": 28, "effect": "+1 weapon proficiency"}, {"level": 35, "effect": "+1 weapon proficiency"}]}, {"name": "Aquatic Adaptation", "description": "Breathe in Deep Sea realm, hold breath 2x in water, move through water without penalty", "type": "passive"}, {"name": "Submerged Weapon Feat", "description": "Choose one level 5 weapon feat usable when submerged/drenched", "type": "passive", "choice_required": true, "condition": "submerged_or_drenched"}]}

fields.name: Spectral Elden
fields.description: Faded ghostlike features with ghost trails. Lose deep/abyssal detection and bonus proficiency. Gain bonus attunement slots and Fade ability.
fields.new_abilities: {"special_abilities": [{"name": "Bonus Attunement Slots", "description": "Bonus attunement slot at levels 3, 7, 13, 19, 27", "type": "passive", "scaling": [{"level": 3, "effect": "+1 attunement slot"}, {"level": 7, "effect": "+1 attunement slot"}, {"level": 13, "effect": "+1 attunement slot"}, {"level": 19, "effect": "+1 attunement slot"}, {"level": 27, "effect": "+1 attunement slot"}]}, {"name": "Fade", "description": "Become intangible/transparent for 2 FP until next turn (5 sec outside combat): immune to grapple/physical damage, pass through inorganic material (\u22643 ft), +3 stealth", "type": "active", "cost": "2 FP", "usage": "2 uses per long rest", "scaling": [{"level": 17, "effect": "3 uses per long rest"}]}]}

fields.name: Mercurous Vessel (Ferno)
fields.description: Prior lineage: Ferno. Inherits Sanity +1 and auto-detect undying stacks.
fields.new_abilities: {"skill_bonuses": {"Sanity": 1}, "special_abilities": [{"name": "Auto-detect Undying", "description": "Automatically detect undying stacks on visible creatures", "type": "passive"}]}

fields.name: Mercurous Vessel (Grme)
fields.description: Prior lineage: Grme. Inherits Precision +1 and craft 2x faster.
fields.new_abilities: {"skill_bonuses": {"Precision": 1}, "special_abilities": [{"name": "Craft Speed", "description": "Craft spells and equipment 2x faster", "type": "passive"}]}

fields.name: Mercurous Vessel (Darrow)
fields.description: Prior lineage: Darrow. Can pinpoint creatures 15 ft via hearing/smell. Darkvision capped at 15 ft.
fields.new_abilities: {"vision": {"type": "blind_with_senses", "range": 15, "special": "Darkvision capped at 15 ft"}}

fields.name: Mercurous Vessel (Corvian)
fields.description: Prior lineage: Corvian. Inherits Acrobatics +1 and 1 FP regen/hour.
fields.new_abilities: {"skill_bonuses": {"Acrobatics": 1}, "regeneration": {"fp": {"base": "1 FP per hour"}}}

fields.name: Mercurous Vessel (Locust-Folk)
fields.description: Prior lineage: Locust-Folk. Knowledge not random, +2 random knowledge in mid/late life. 1d4 HP die.
fields.new_abilities: {"hp_modifier": {"die_override": "1d4"}, "special_abilities": [{"name": "Knowledge Bonus", "description": "Knowledge not random, +2 random knowledge in mid/late life", "type": "passive"}]}

fields.name: Mercurous Vessel (Dragonkind)
fields.description: Prior lineage: Dragonkind. 1 tier magic OR fire resistance, OR +3 poison OR frost threshold.
fields.new_abilities: {"special_abilities": [{"name": "Choose Elemental Affinity", "description": "Choose ONE: 1 tier fire resistance OR 1 tier magic resistance OR +3 poison threshold OR +3 frost threshold", "type": "passive", "choice_required": true}]}

fields.name: Mercurous Vessel (Demonkind)
fields.description: Prior lineage: Demonkind. Fire Keeping +1, Demonkind retaliation once/short rest (1d6 magic damage).
fields.new_abilities: {"skill_bonuses": {"Firekeeping": 1}, "special_abilities": [{"name": "Melee Retaliation", "description": "1d6 magic damage to melee attacker", "type": "passive", "usage": "once/short_rest"}]}

fields.name: Mercurous Vessel (Unchanged Human)
fields.description: Prior lineage: Unchanged Human. +1 to a stat.
fields.new_abilities: {"special_abilities": [{"name": "Flexible Stat", "description": "+1 to any stat (player choice)", "type": "passive", "choice_required": true}]}

fields.name: Mercurous Vessel (Vileblood)
fields.description: Prior lineage: Vileblood. Sanity -1, 13 base bleed threshold, can extend life stages 2x duration.
fields.new_abilities: {"skill_bonuses": {"Sanity": -1}, "thresholds": {"bleed": 13}, "special_abilities": [{"name": "Extend Life Stages", "description": "Can extend life stages 2x duration", "type": "passive"}]}

fields.name: Mercurous Vessel (Elden)
fields.description: Prior lineage: Elden. Athletics -1, -1 starting HP, +1 weapon proficiency at levels 5, 13, 23.
fields.new_abilities: {"skill_bonuses": {"Athletics": -1}, "hp_modifier": {"flat": -1}, "special_abilities": [{"name": "Weapon Proficiency Scaling", "description": "+1 weapon proficiency at levels 5, 13, 23", "type": "passive", "scaling": [{"level": 5, "effect": "+1 weapon proficiency"}, {"level": 13, "effect": "+1 weapon proficiency"}, {"level": 23, "effect": "+1 weapon proficiency"}]}]}

fields.name: Accurate
fields.description: Whenever you hit a creature with a precision attack, the next precision attack you make on them has advantage. Increase your precision by +2.

fields.name: Advanced Legwork
fields.description: Every turn in combat you may take 1 AP worth of free movement on your turn. At character level 15 this becomes 2 AP of free movement. The free movement given by this feat may be used before resolving a slow action such as a heavy attack or charged spell.

fields.name: Armorer
fields.description:
Your expertise with armors causes each type of armor to give you one more tier of physical damage reduction (light now gives one tier, medium two, and heavy three). Wearing heavy armor now also gives (Vit mod) bonus max HP, +1 poise threshold, and the penalty to stealth and acrobatics is reduced to -3 each. Gain (Vit Mod) to all status resistances (not including poise threshold) when wearing medium armor, and no longer take a penalty to stealth. Gain (1 + Vit mod/2) Max FP when wearing light armor. You now have advantage on attunement checks to identify the properties of unknown armor.

In addition, over the course of a long rest you may now spend 4-5 days of hard work (2-3 days if you are a Grme, but still only once per long rest) near a magical bonfire/resting area to add an innate tier 0 property to your own or an allies armor (as long as the armor doesn't already have an innate property). See the "Armor bonus properties and tiers" section of the "Combat" chapter for more info on innate properties and for a list of properties of each tier. Over that same period of time you may instead fully repair an armor, removing any normal durability damage it has sustained.

fields.name: Artisan of Physic
fields.description:
From studying the effect of a wide variety of curiosities and materials you have become adept at creating wonderous custom medicines. Over the course of a long rest you may spend typlically 4-5 days of hard work (2-3 days if you are a Grme, but still only once per long rest) near a magical bonfire/resting area to create a number of potions and medicines, by using the magic of the bonfire/resting place. Note that any potion or medicine you create through this feat counts as an item with appropriate AP cost to use in battle (based on Dexterity stat). The number of potions you can create over this time depends on your character level and the type of potion you are crafting. During each crafting session, every character level gives you one artisan point which you may spend to craft the following type of consumables:

**Blood Extraction:** (1 artisan point each, up to a maximum of 3 per crafting session) → Extract and process your own blood to be used in a pinch. Roll a d2, if the result is a 2 then successfully process the blood and create a blood vial item. If the result is a 1 then the blood congeals and separates creating a crimson water. See the items list for details on the blood vial and crimson water items.

**Material Potions:** (2 artisan points each) → Brew a mix of a drop of your own blood, and materials such as plants and monster parts to create a drink or consumable with a useful effect. Does not cost many artisan points, but each one requires specific separate materials which must be collected.

A list of material potions and their ingredients:

Minor potion of frost resistance (requires → bellowing snowbloom, or rime blue moss bulb): Drink to gain +3 frost status resistance for next four hours.

Potion of frost resistance (requires → frost resistant monster flesh): Drink to gain +6 frost status resistance for next four hours.

Cream of frost cure (requires → great tree root resin, pristine ice, and potent herbs): Apply to wounds when poisoned to cure the poisoned status effect but take 1d4 frost build up. The first time this is used to cure a creatures poison, that creature gains a permanent +2 frost resistance.

Minor potion of blood clotting (requires → monster blood clot, or bloodred moss bulb): Drink to gain +3 bleed status resistance for the next four hours.

Potion of blood clotting (requires → unclotted and cold beast blood): Drink to gain +6 bleed status resistance for the next four hours.

Cream of blood warmth (requires → great tree root resin, razor tulip, and potent herbs): Apply to wounds when frostbitten to cure the frostbitten status effect but take 1d4 bleed build up. The first time this is used to cure a creatures frostbite, that creature gains a permanent +2 bleed resistance.

Minor potion of elemental resistance (requires → colourful flowers and dense coal (fire), or crystal residue (magic), or gold flower (lightning), or dried sane human flesh (dark)): Drink to increase elemental damage resistance by one tier for four hours. The elemental resistance gained corresponds to the element of the potion that was brewed.

Potion of elemental resistance (requires → colourful flowers and mantle coal (fire), or white blood (magic), or fulgur steel (lightning), or living human bone powder (dark)): Drink to increase elemental damage resistance by two tiers for four hours. The elemental resistance gained corresponds to the element of the potion that was brewed.

Balm of embetterment (requires → dense coal, gold, and the most pristine organ of a fast monster (Endurance), or wise monster (Faith), or intelligent monster (Intelligence), or strong monster (Strength), or dextrous monster (Dexterity), or focused monster (Attunement)): Apply the warm balm to your skin to increase a stat by one for the next hour. The increased stat depends on the pristine organ ingredient used in brewing.

Companion restoration potion (requires → mercury residue and a blessed mushroom): Drink to revive a magical companion (as per the Companion destined trait) which was destroyed when within 60 feet of you. The companion is restored to half HP and FP, and appears in an unoccupied space next to you, or in your space if it is your own turn and they can move out of the space on their own.

Custom rejuvenation potion (requires → dried herbs, a blessed mushroom, and a prism stone): Upon creation of this potion, allocate ten points into either HP or FP regeneration. When this potion is drank it restores the allocated amount of HP and FP.

Potion of translucence (requires → mercury residue, crystal residue, and a silken cloth): Drink to gain the non charged effects of the Hidden Body spell on yourself.

Broth of ephemeral tongues (requires → mercury residue and a living humans tongue): Consume the thick broth to gain the ability to fluently speak the racial language of the person whos tongue was used in brewing (Grme, Fernian, Corvian, Draconic, Demonic, Darrish, Abyssal (Locust speak), Deep speak (Elden), Lethrellian (Vileblood/Ancient)). This effect lasts for four hours after consumption.

Green bulb tea (requires → greenblossom bulb (x4), or blooming greenblossom bulb (x2)): A very pleasant tea which revitalizes the body. Drink to increase the AP of your next two turns in combat by 3. This effect does not stack with itself or that of raw greenblossom or blooming greenblossom bulbs.

Purple bulb tea (requires → purple moss bulb (x3) and a blooming purple moss bulb): A strong and pungent tea which strengthens the bodies response to poisons. Drink to cure both poisoned and badly poisoned statuses, and the first time it is used to cure them, permanently increase your status threshold for poison and toxic by +2 (for curing poisoned and badly poisoned statuses respectively).

**Incense Jars:** (3 artisan points each) → A strange mix of expanding gasses and particles which can often be sprayed over an area of multiple grid spaces. These medicines can give buffs to yourself and multiple allies, but often come with negative side effects. Does not require any external materials for creation. Ten days after they are made, incense jars lose their potency and effects, becoming a pale vapour with little to no value.

A list of incense jars and their effects:

Sizzling Bubbles: Toss the jar of fumes into the air around you to coat yourself and up to two willing allies of your choice, within 15 feet of yourself, with sizzling sparks of repelling force. All affected creatures gain three tiers of physical resistance, but also the impaired vision status condition, until the start of your following turn.

Bloodrage Aroma: Spray blood red fumes in a cone reaching up to 15 feet in front of yourself, or directly into your own face. You, or any willing allies within the area you sprayed, may breath deep of the fumes to enrage themselves until the start of your next turn. Each enraged creature may add two d20 damage die to any two attacks they make (with the damage type being the same as the majority damage of the attack), but they are also inflicted by the berserk status condition forcing them to no longer make the distinction between friend or foe over the same period of time.

Petrification Perfume: Pop open this jar of incense to allow yourself and one willing ally within 5 feet of you to breath deep of its fumes. You both become inflicted with the staggered status condition, but are also immune to any non true damage until the condition clears. In this state you are frozen in position and cannot resist any hostile effects or forced movements.

Clinging Bell Fume: Toss the jar of metallic particles into the air around you to coat yourself and up to three willing allies of your choice, within 15 feet of yourself, with small vibrating strands or metal. All affected creatures are inflicted with the deafened status condition by the vibrations, but gain the ability to easily cling to and climb any surface (for regular AP cost of movement), until the start of your following turn.

Flowered Spice: A wonderfully scented incense which is a pleasure to imbibe. Any creature exposed to this scent gains a heightened sense of focus, but also a dependence on the substance for a short time. Use on yourself, a willing ally, or a restrained creature which cannot hold its breath for long, to give them +1 FP regeneration on the start of their turns in combat and +5 to perception and precision for ten minutes. When this effect subsides the creature must use another dosage of this incense within the next hour or gain a point of exhaustion.

Incense of Rebirth: Toss these crystalline fumes over a small area during a bonfire/magical short rest to allow up to two willing and adjacent creatures to raise one stat of their choice, and lower another. This incense jar will only affect a creature the first time it is used on them, and gives them a permanent paler and more sickly complexion.

Chaotic Rejuvenation Mists: Spray the jar of mist into the air around you to coat up to three willing creatures of your choice, within 10 feet of you, in a rainbow dew. Each affected creature affected must roll (2d10 - 8). If the result is positive then they may distribute the number into HP and FP regeneration as they see fit. If the result is negative then they must take its magnitude in true damage or FP loss as they see fit. If the result is zero then they may permanently increase their maximum HP by two (up to a maximum possible +6 max HP from this incense jar).

Chaotic Status Mists: Spray the jar of mist into the air around you to coat up to three willing creatures of your choice, within 10 feet of you, in a prismatic dew of a specific (non-poise) status effect which was chosen upon the creation of this incense jar. Each affected creature must roll (2d8 - 7). If the result is positive then they may reduce their build up of the status effect by the same amount. If the result is negative then they must take its magnitude in status build up of the specified status effect. If the result is zero then they may permanently increase their maximum status threshold for that status effect by one (up to a maximum possible +3 max status threshold per status from this incense jar).

Mimic's Veiled Fog: Spray the jar of vapours into the air around you to coat up to three willing creatures of your choice, within 10 feet of you, in an ever changing fog. Each creature affected gains the non charged effects of the sorcery spell Powdered Light, but for only twelve seconds (two rounds of combat). When the duration elapses all affected creatures are inflicted with the dazed status condition for six seconds (one round in combat).

Ignition Powder: Spray a sparking powder in a cone reaching up to 15 feet in front of yourself, which then ignites and burns as an item based attack. All creatures which are hit by the burning powder take (2d4 + D Str, D Dex) fire damage, and 1d2 durability loss to their equipped armor or innate physical protection.

Jagged Crystal Powder: Spray a mist of jagged crystalline powder in a cone reaching up to 15 feet in front of yourself, which crackles and cuts the area as an item based attack. All creatures which are hit by the crystal powder take (2d4 + D Str, D Dex) magic damage, and 1d2 bleed build up.

Heavy Powder: Spray a fog of heavy gravitational powder in a cone reaching up to 15 feet in front of yourself, which falls and causes the earth to spike up from the area as an item based attack. All grounded creatures which are hit by the spiked earth take (2d4 + D Str, D Dex) physical damage. After the attack is resolved, the area of spiked earth becomes difficult terrain as the spikes crumble.

**Wonderous Concoctions:** (5 artisan points each) → An expertly mixed concoction providing custom effects when drank. Each one is a mix of two beneficial properties of your choosing. Does not require any external materials for creation, but a creature may not experience the effects of a second wonderous concoction until they finish their next bonfire/magical long rest. Ten days after they are made, all wonderous concoctions will dilute, causing them to lose their effects and become an oily liquid with little to no value.

Choose up to two of any of these effects when creating a wonderous concoction:

- Set all damage resistances to 90% reduction, until after the next instance of damage you take, or until one minute has elapsed (ten rounds of combat).
- The next instance of elemental damage you take, of an element of choice, instead heals you for the amount of damage you would have taken (not including any damage resistances you may have had, and with the choice of element being made upon brewing the concoction).
- Gain the ability to cast spells for no FP cost until the start of your next turn after drinking (about 6 seconds). Although, any AP cost reduction to casting these spells does not apply while this effect is active.
- Increase your innate speed, allowing you to immediately start a sprint while retaining the ability to make any other action until the end of your turn.
- Increase your maximum HP by 20% (but do not heal for that amount). This effect is prioritized to take place before any other effect of the concoction, and lasts for one minute (ten rounds of combat).
- Heal for 50% of your maximum HP.
- Increase your maximum FP by 25% (but do not replenish it for that amount). This effect is prioritized to take place before any other effect of the concoction, and lasts for one minute (ten rounds of combat).
- Regenerate 50% of your maximum FP.
- Increase your maximum AP by one, for one minute (ten rounds of combat).
- Increase your maximum poise threshold by 2, for one minute (ten rounds of combat).
- Swap your status effect thresholds for two status effects (not including poise). This effect lasts for one minute (ten rounds of combat).
- Heal 1 HP for every point of status build up you reduce on yourself (including over time reduction of build up). This effect lasts for one minute (ten rounds of combat).
- Reduce your status build up for a chosen non poise status to zero (with the choice of status made upon brewing the concoction).
- Every attack you make until the start of your next turn (after resolving any slow actions), deals a bonus +1 damage which increases by 1 for each attack you make.
- The next heavy attack you make deals +2 poise damage. This effect dissipates after one minute (ten rounds of combat).
- If you die from having your HP reduced to 0, until the end of your next turn, you may add +3 to your undying check.
- The next skill check you make to resist an adverse effect in combat, automatically succeeds. This effect dissipates after one minute (ten rounds of combat).
- Explode, dealing 2d10 fire damage to all grid spaces within 10 feet of yourself, but deal the same amount in true damage to yourself. This effect is prioritized to take place after any other effect of the concoction.

fields.name: Athlete
fields.description: You may now climb most surfaces and swim through normal water without any speed penalty (although you still might need to make a skill check to determine if you can climb a surface). You may now also jump 5 feet farther from a standing position, and attempt to grapple an adjacent creature with your hands for only 2AP (instead of 3). Your vertical jump AP cost is now reduced from 3 to 2.

fields.name: Bloodthirsty
fields.description: You are a endless vessel for souls and can pull forth more souls than usual. Whenever you gain souls from defeating enemies in combat, you now gain 5% more souls (rounded down). Lower the souls you need for each level up by 10%.

fields.name: Companion
fields.description:
You have a permanent pet or small monster companion that is magically bound to you and an extension of your person. In battle it acts on your turn. The companion can communicate basic feeling to you through sound or line of sight, and you can give them verbal or non verbal commands. If your companion dies they re spawn the next time you take a short rest at a bonfire. They can move away from you to any range, but if they are killed outside of a 60 foot range of you then they will not re spawn until your next long rest, instead of your next short rest.

Their resources and attacks depend on your stats, and are as follows; HP: 10 + (5 × V itMod) + (character level), AP: 2 + ( 1/2× your max AP), FP: 1 + ( 1/2× your max FP), Basic Attack: costs 2 AP and deals 1d4 + C Str (or Dex, your choice) to an adjacent space, and with a heavy attack that deals two more damage die and two poise damage, but costs one more AP as usual. Your companions attack scaling increases by one letter grade at character levels 10 and 20.

Your companion is immune to status effects, and their skills are all zero with two non-knowledge skills of your choice starting at +2. At character level 9 you may increase those two skills to +4 and choose two more non-knowledge skills to be +2. At character level 19 you may increase the +4 skills to +6.

Your companion may have one special ability that they can use a maximum of once per round on your turn, costing 1 FP. This ability can either give one medium or smaller creature within 5 feet of them the impaired vision status condition for one round (this ability costs 2 FP instead), heal an ally for 1d4 + (your Att mod) HP at a range of 60 feet (increasing to 1d6 at level 10 and 1d8 at level 20), take 50% reduced physical damage from the next attack they are hit by that round, or add 1d2 + (your character level/10) status damage to all of their attacks for the round (status of your choice excluding curse, toxic, and poise damage). Your companion can dodge a distance of 5 feet, once per round, for a 2 AP cost. This feat can be taken multiple times.

fields.name: Cook
fields.description:
Once per long rest you may spend 1-2 days of hard work to prepare up to (character level/3) delicious meals for yourself and allies. These delicious meals may heal a number of d6 in HP equal to half of your character level, add a number of d2 in temporary HP equal to one third of your character level (until the next bonfire short rest), and give advantage on the next vitality check made until the next bonfire short rest. These meals take one hour of rest to fully consume and digest, and once someone has consumed one they may not benefit from the effects of another until they finish a short rest. The meals expire at the end of your next long rest.

As well, you may use any local ingredients and your cooking skills to make sure that you and up to four other allies have been fed well enough to not gain any points of exhaustion from hunger.

fields.name: Craftsperson
fields.description:
You are a skilled craftsperson that is well acquainted with creating useful tools and items by expertly using only few materials. Over the course of a long rest you may spend typically 4-5 days of hard work (2-3 days if you are a Grme, but still only once per long rest) near a magical bonfire/resting area to to craft a number of items, by using the magic of the bonfire/resting place. The maximum number of items you may create during your crafting time is equal to (1 + (your character level/5)). Each item requires some specific materials to create and the details of their effects can be found in the Items list section. The recipes of craftable items are as follows (where each entry with a multiplier, such as x5 or x10, counts as one item for the sake of your crafting):

Prism Stone (x5) → small round stone + throat flesh + colourful flowers
Light bugs → common fireflies + fulgur steel
Purging Stone → pure abyssal crystal + glimmering stone + innocent human faces (x20) + siderite chunk
Blessed Mushroom (x10) → mushroom + miracle spell casting implement
Dried Greatfeather (x10) → large bird feather + dense coal
Bellowing snowbloom → snowbloom flower + bottled strong wind
Divine Blessing → miracle spell casting implement + priest's ring + gold
Throwing Pots → round pot + oil (Firebomb)/black powder (Black Firebomb)/fulgur steel (Lightning Pot)/crystal residue (Mage's Pot)/mercury residue (Sleep Pot)
Status Knives → throwing knives + acid (Acidic throwing knives)/razor tulip (Lacerating throwing knives)/snowbloom flower (Frosted throwing knives)/poison gland (Poison throwing knives)
Arrows/Bolts (x10) → quality wood, or small beast bones + bird feather
Great Arrows/Bolts (x5) → large quality wood, or large beast bones + large bird feather
Elemental Arrows/Bolts (x10) → arrows/bolts (x10) + crystal residue (Magic)/gold flower (Lightning)/dense coal (Fire)/dried sane human flesh (Dark)
Status Arrows/Bolts (x10) → arrows/bolts (x10) + razor tulip (Bleed)/snowbloom flower (Frost)/poison gland (Poison)/poison dragon mold (Toxic)
Elemental Great Arrows/Bolts (x5) → great arrows/bolts (x5) + white blooded monster skin (Magic)/fulgur steel (Lightning)/mantle coal (Fire)/living human bone powder (Dark)
Status Great Arrows/Bolts (x5) → great arrows/bolts (x5) + large beasts blood (Bleed)/pristine ice (Frost)/large poison gland (Poison)/poison dragon mold (Toxic)
Status Pastes → moss slime + blood clot (Rouge paste)/rotten flesh (Rot paste)/pristine ice (Cobalt paste)
Element Resins → great tree root resin + white blood (Pale resin)/gold flower (Gold resin)/dense coal (Charcoal resin)/dried sane human flesh (Human resin)
Pure infusion stones → great tree root resin + glimmering stone + white blooded monster flesh (Magic)/fulgur steel (Lightning)/mantle coal (Fire)/living human bone powder (Dark)
Bug Pellets → dried monster liver + large insect head + crystal residue (Blue bug pellet)/gold flower (Yellow bug pellet)/dense coal (Red bug pellet)
Orange Soapstone → spirit egg stone + monster fat + gold flower
Stone of gentle warmth → round stone + dense coal + fireproof monster part
Duel charm → steaming hot tears + gold
Sand trap → hot desert sand + unclotted cold beast blood
Drain capsule → bottled strong wind + dried herbs + spongy monster part
Numbing mist → steaming hot tears + potent herbs + acid
Repair powder → gold + crystal residue + powdered glass
Rope (x feet) → x feet of vines or reeds
Torch → cloth + oil

fields.name: Critical Rolls
fields.description: Once per round, any time one of your weapon attacks has one or more of its damage die roll maximum damage, you may add two more of that damage die to that attack as bonus damage. If any of the bonus damage die also roll maximum damage you may add ONE more bonus damage die to those rolls as well, continuing to add bonus damage die until they no longer roll maximum damage. For example, attacking with a greataxe and rolling a 12 on one of its d12 damage die gives you a bonus of two more d12 damage to that attack. If either of those two bonus d12 also roll a 12 then you may add another bonus d12 damage for each one that did. This continues until you no longer roll maximum damage on the bonus die. increase your precision skill by one.

fields.name: Cultivate Mass
fields.description: You now roll 1d10 to increase your health at every even level. Taking this feat allows characters at level two or higher to reset their HP back to the base level dictated by their starting background (plus any other HP bonuses they might have) and re roll all of their even level HP increases.

fields.name: Delay Death
fields.description:
You embrace being undead. When your health drops to zero you may choose not to die. When you choose to stay alive at zero HP any damage will now start to mangle you instead, giving you permanent injuries and reducing your effectiveness in battle (based on the attack you take, up to GM's discretion). These injuries persists until your head is destroyed or you choose to end your suffering and die. Some example injuries effects are:

Body injuries → lower max AP, recover half as much AP, lower max HP, lower effectiveness of healing on you, lower poise threshold.

Head injuries → drastically lower perception, loss of the ability to speak, hear, or see, lower max FP or effectiveness of FP regeneration.

Leg injuries → drastically lower acrobatics or athletics, double movement costs, prevent dodging, prevent movement actions at all, take more poise damage.

Arm injuries → drastically lower acrobatics or athletics, lose the use of an arm, forced precision check when attacking.

Other injuries from specific attacks → permanent lowering of resistances, permanent status effect (frostbitten, bled out, poisoned, badly poisoned).

fields.name: Deliberate Items
fields.description:
You may now use multiple items on yourself as a slow action, for your regular item usage AP cost (reducible by other feats which reduce that cost). These items must be "consumables" (i.e. have the word consume or drink in their description), and you may only use a maximum of three at once (including drinking multiple charges of a recovery flask). Although, if you take damage or are moved before the action resolves then it is interrupted. You must declare what you are consuming when the slow action is initiated, but you don't have to decide how many charges/multiples of the item you are consuming until the action resolves.

You may also now use throwing items as slow actions (items or tools with the word "throw" or "thrown" in their description). This allows them to be thrown so that they hit the first creature in a line up to their maximum range, increases their range by one grid space (5 feet), and allows damaging throwing items to deal two extra damage die of damage.

fields.name: Desperation Dive
fields.description:
Increase the maximum distance you can dodge by one grid space (5 feet). You now have the choice to make a desperation dive as a reaction to an attack instead of a basic dodge action. A desperation dive is a dodge which can travel up to twice as far, doubling your dodge distance and increasing the cap to 6 grid spaces (30 feet), but knocks you prone and causes you to be unable to take any action until the end of your next turn.

Desperation dives can be used to push willing allies away from danger. This halts your dodge and knocks you prone as usual, but pushes your ally the remaining distance of your dive, in the same direction. A desperation dive cannot be made when you are prone, and cannot be made as a reckless dodge (as described in the Reckless Dodge dagger weapon feat).

fields.name: Durable
fields.description: Increase your Maximum HP by 5 and your Vitality stat by 2.

fields.name: Elemental
fields.description: Upon taking this feat you may permanently gain one tier of resistance to either magic, fire, or lightning damage. Getting hit by that type of element also regenerates your FP by 1. Gain +1 to your attunement stat. At level 17 you may take an additional tier of resistance, in the same element or different (from magic, fire, or lightning). If you choose a different element it also regenerates your FP by one when you are struck by it. Note that if you are hit by an element you choose, and somehow take no damage, then it will only regenerate your FP if you are in combat when hit.

fields.name: Fast Roll
fields.description: You may take an additional dodge action per round of combat. Increase your acrobatics by 2.

fields.name: Fire Clairvoyant
fields.description:
Staring into chaotic magical flames has granted you the gift of knowing what will come to pass through gaining stacks of clairvoyance. (1 + (Fire Keeping/3)) times per short rest when a creature other than yourself is about to perform a skill check you may declare whether it will be a success or failure before the roll is made as long as the DC is at least 8, gaining a stack of clairvoyance upon a successful prediction. Alternatively you may declare one participant a victor or loser in the case of a skill contest as long as the creatures are hostile towards each other. If there was only one possible outcome of a prediction the GM will notify you that your prediction has automatically failed. Once you have made a prediction you must complete a short rest before you can make another prediction about that same creature. All clairvoyance stacks expire upon completing a short rest.

You now have the choice to make a perfect dodge as a reaction to an attack instead of a basic dodge action by expending a stack of clairvoyance. When performing a perfect dodge, choose a valid location within range of your current dodge distance, if the chosen location is not targeted by the attack you avoid all incoming damage of the attack and may choose your final location to be either the chosen location or your starting location. If a perfect dodge succeeds then its AP cost is 0. If you successfully make a perfect dodge and choose to remain in your starting location you are treated as not having made a movement action for the purpose of things such as breaking a slow action.

You may spend one stack of clairvoyance to capture the final result of another creatures die roll, as long as you could see the creature and the result has not already been captured or forced. The next time that creature makes the same type of die roll, if you can see them, you may force them to use the result that you captured. You can only capture one die roll at a time, you may not make clairvoyance predictions on results that have been forced, and you may not capture die results that have been forced. Any captured die expire upon completing a short rest, or if the creature makes the check again and you did not force the result.

A stack of clairvoyance may also be expended to reduce the FP cost of the following spells to 0: Translucence, Unveil, Empathy, Lithomancy, Pyrovoyance.

fields.name: Focus Armor
fields.description: As a reaction when you about to receive any non-true, non self inflicted damage, you may reduce that damage by an amount equal to (Xd10 + Att mod), by spending 2*X FP to generate an internal barrier of magical focus. For example, spending 4 FP allows you create a focus barrier which reduces the damage you are about to receive by 2d10 plus your Att mod. You may use this damage reducing ability after you see the amount of damage you are about to take, and it can be used (Att mod) times per bonfire short rest. Regenerate 1d2 + (Att mod/2) FP on the start of your turn, on any round of combat where you used the damage reducing ability of this feat and took no damage.

fields.name: Free of Burden
fields.description: Only the starting background "Deprived" may choose this feat. Start with any two pieces of starting equipment. Your total equip load is double the normal amount. Increase your strength stat and your maximum poise threshold by one.

fields.name: Haggler
fields.description: Increase your diplomacy skill by 2. You may always purchase things from NPCs for 20% fewer souls or money.

fields.name: Hidden Bobby
fields.description: You may now spend 1 FP to summon Hidden Bobby at any time. Hidden Bobby may or may not exist.

fields.name: Human Skill
fields.description: Gain +3 Precision, Acrobatics, and Stealth. These bonuses increase to +4 at level 7, +5 at level 13, +6 at level 19, and +7 at level 24. Each stack of undying lowers these bonuses by one (they can be negative if undying is high enough).

fields.name: Innate Spell
fields.description: Choose one of the spells Great Soul Arrow, Cold Burst, Fire Orb, or Med Heal to be an innate spell of yours. You gain the spell if you do not already have it. At character level 3 it also no longer takes up an attunement slot. At character level 5, in combat, you can cast the innate spell for one additional AP, but no FP. At level 9 you gain +1 fire keeping if you chose Fire Orb, +1 precision if you chose Great Soul Arrow, +1 stealth if you chose Cold Burst, and +1 diplomacy if you chose Med Heal. At character level 13 and above, the first time you cast the spell each combat so that it costs no FP, you instead regain 1 FP.

fields.name: Library of Knowledge (Skills)
fields.description: Gain knowledge and learn any two skills from the following list: Shield Splitter, Barricade Shield, Phantom Slash, Wild Swings, Rotted Spores, Perseverance, Gravity's Reinforcement, Galvanize, Banner of Inspiration, Airblade Slice, Follow Through, Piercing Shot/Attack, Back Attack, Life Drain, Assassin's Blood Sand, White Shadow Mist, Illusory Darts, Nightbird's Call, Spinning Weapon, Item Juggler, Flaming Cascade, Water Body, Lightning Tumble, Necromantic Chant. At character level 13 and 17 learn another skill from the list. Gain three knowledge points and +1 to your attunement stat.

fields.name: Library of Knowledge (Spells)
fields.description: Gain knowledge of any two spells with all requirements strictly lower than 14. At character level 13 learn two more spells with all requirements that are 15 or lower. At character level 23 learn two more spells with all requirements that are 17 or lower. Gain three knowledge points and +1 to your attunement stat.

fields.name: Limitless
fields.description: Increase your maximum character level to 35, and the maximum possible value of two stats to 22. This feat can be invested into at any point while leveling a character.

fields.name: Linguist
fields.description: You are fluent in one additional racial language (Grme, Fernian, Corvian, Draconic, Demonic, Darrish, Abyssal (Locust speak), Deep Speech (Elden), Lethrellian (Vileblood/Ancient)), as well as one majority language from your country or a neighboring country. Increase your perception by +2, and gain a knowledge point. Once per short rest you may focus deeply to expertly adapt your gestures and words to another creature, allowing them to understand what you wish to communicate to them as long as you can describe your communication in 25 words or less. This is possible as long as the creature can actively perceive you (visually, audibly, touch, taste etc.), regardless of if you share a language or normal form of communication. At character level 10 and 20 you gain an additional use of this ability per bonfire short rest.

fields.name: Lord of Undeath
fields.description: You have advantage on undying checks. Each stack of undying increases your maximum HP by 5.

fields.name: Lucky
fields.description: Rolling a natural 20 on any non knowledge skill roll has a DC 10 chance to permanently increase that skill by 1. Each time this feat increases a skill the DC chance to permanently increase that skill increases by one, up to a maximum DC of 20. So, for example, if you have increased your perception 5 times through this feat then the next time you roll a natural 20 on a perception check, you need to make a DC 15 on another d20 roll to add another +1 to your perception with this feat.

fields.name: Mage Slayer
fields.description: When a creature within melee range of you attempts to cast a spell or use a spirit summon you may make a free weapon light attack on them as a reaction. Gain one tier of resistance to the last element you were struck with. If you are hit with a weapon that is buffed by a spell, the spell is removed.

fields.name: Malformed Aspect
fields.description: You can only take this trait when creating a new character. You are afflicted by an aspect of the crucible, and must choose to have a malformed non demonic affliction that is either: white horns growing from your head, a malformed white tail, or malformed gray wings (that cannot be used to fly normally). Upon taking this feat learn the Horn Growth Aspect spell, then at character levels 5, and 12 learn the Tail, and Wing Growth Aspect spells respectively. When learned through this feat, these spells may be attuned without needing to spend spell slots or meet the stat requirements, and can be cast with no casting implement, but their normal scaling is replaced by (your character level/5). If you cast the spells normally with a casting implement and meeting their requirements, then they each deal damage at one die level greater (d6 becomes d8, d8 becomes d10) and you may choose to use their normal scaling. Decrease your diplomacy skill by 1.

fields.name: Mobile
fields.description: You are no longer affected by difficult terrain. You may now run and climb along walls, without needing a skill check, at half normal speed (twice the AP cost). Gain advantage on all acrobatics checks.

fields.name: Multi Proficient
fields.description: Gain a tertiary weapon proficiency point every three levels, up to level 30 (gaining a total of ten). Tertiary weapon proficiency points can only be spent on a weapon tree that is less than or equal to your two most proficient weapon types.

fields.name: Observant
fields.description: Increase your perception by 2. Whenever a beast or non verbal person is trying to communicate with you, you may perceive their intentions with great ease. You have advantage on any checks to identify properties of armor/rings and content of spellbooks, as well as the ability to read lips.

fields.name: Power Funnel
fields.description: Permanently lower one of your positive skills by an amount X which does not make the skill negative, then increase your maximum health by 2.5X and increase a different non-knowledge skill by X/2. The number X cannot be chosen be greater than 10.

fields.name: Prosthetic Limb
fields.description:
This feat can only be taken when making a new character, or during a suitable moment in RP (up to GM's discretion). Replace a missing arm or leg with a mechanical prosthetic made up of gears, rope, metal, and hard wood. The prosthetic limb bestows you with new abilities and enhancements based on if it is an arm or a leg. The limb can take durability damage and if a prosthetic limb would have normally been inflicted with limb fracture, it instead takes 1d6 durability damage. At 5 durability damage a prosthetic can no longer be used for its special tools and abilities, and at 10 durability damage it is permanently affected by the limb fracture condition until it is fully repaired.

**Prosthetic Arm**

Costs 2 points of fate. Over the course of a long rest you may spend typically 4-5 days of precise work (2-3 days if you are a Grme, but still only once per long rest) near a magical bonfire/resting area to equip your prosthetic arm with a number of useful tools and contraptions based on your character level. Each time you equip your prosthetic, any tools and contraptions it contains are replaced with the new ones you choose. During each prosthetic equipping session, every character level gives you one prosthetic tool point to be spent on the following prosthetic tools:

Shuriken (x5 for 2 prosthetic tool points) → Small shuriken which can be thrown up to 20 feet toward a target space to deal (1d4 + E Dex) physical damage. Using a shuriken costs AP equal to your item usage cost and counts as a throwing knife for the sake of bonuses from feats.

Elemental Shuriken (x5 for 3 prosthetic tool points) → Small shuriken which can be thrown up to 20 feet to deal (1d4 + E Dex) fire, lightning, or magic damage to a target space in range. The element of each batch must be decided when this tool is equipped. Using an elemental shuriken costs AP equal to your item usage cost as well as 1 FP, and counts as a throwing knife for the sake of bonuses from feats.

Hatchet (x3 for 2 prosthetic tool points) → Small hatchets which can be thrown up to 20 feet toward a target space to deal (1d6 + C Str) physical damage. Each hatchet costs 3 AP to use.

Status Hatchet (x3 for 3 prosthetic tool points) → Small hatchets which can be thrown up to 20 feet toward a target space to deal (1d6 + D Str) physical damage and 2d4 bleed, frost, or poison status build up. The status type of each batch must be decided when this tool is equipped. Each status hatchet costs 3 AP as well as 1 FP to use.

Overheat Charge (x1 for 4 prosthetic tool points) → Build in a powerful cartridge which allows you to overheat the prosthetic and add damage to an attack. On your turn in combat you may expend the cartridge to add a bonus 1d20 damage to a weapon attack, with the weapon wielded by the prosthetic. This bonus damage is the same type as the majority damage of the attack. After the buffed attack, the arm can no longer be used to attack with any weapon or use any additional tools until the end of your next turn. This prosthetic tool may only be used before it is known if your attack hits.

Concealed Weapon (5 prosthetic tool points) → Choose any weapon which you can wield in one hand and engineer a contraption which allows it to fold in and out of your prosthetic. Allows you to spend 2 AP and 1 FP to draw the weapon into your hand or conceal it back into the arm. This does not take up one of your accessible equipment slots, but if the arm is destroyed with a concealed weapon then it cannot be retrieved until the arm is repaired.

Deflection Plate (7 prosthetic tool points) → Allows you, once per round, to spend 3 FP to spring out a deflection plate and use the weapon skill Parry without needing to attune it or wield a weapon which has access to it. This tool is not affected by FP reductions to the usage of Parry itself.

Auto Shield (7 prosthetic tool points) → Spend 3 FP to unfurl a metallic umbrella shield from the prosthetic arm as a reaction, blocking an attack which threatens you from any direction. You may use this tool once per round before an attack hits you, in order to convert the attacks physical damage into poise damage as though you had blocked with a regular shield (including the AP loss from taking poise damage with the shield).
